title: Congresswoman Barbara Lee Marks 2nd Anniversary of No Child Left Behind by Calling on President Bush to Keep Promise to Fully Fund Act
text:
01.08.04
			 Congresswoman Barbara Lee Marks 2nd Anniversary of No Child Left Behind by Calling on President Bush to Keep Promise to Fully Fund Act 
			Oakland,
CA
 Congresswoman
Barbara Lee today called on President Bush to keep his promise to America's
children, teachers and parents by fully funding the No Child Left Behind Act (NCLB),
which was signed into law two years ago today.

Today,
unfortunately, represents the anniversary of a broken promise, said
Lee.  President Bush made a promise to the American people about the
futures of our children, but he has repeatedly undermined that vow.

Last
year alone, the President and the Congress provided $8 billion less than the
legislation than he himself signed. Reports about the forthcoming FY 05 budget
suggest that he will be similarly remiss this year.  The underfunding has
resulted in millions of lost dollars for California's
schools. For the current fiscal year, schools in California will get $3.731
billion less than promised by the law, including:

       
$ 2627.6 million less for aid to poor school
districts through the Title I program;

       
$ 76.7 million less for Impact Aid to school
districts with high percentages of military families;

       
$ 43.3 million less for training and hiring
teachers;

       
$ 47.8 million less for educational technology;

       
$ 24.7 million less for innovative programs to
improve student achievement;

       
$ 28.5 million less for Safe and Drug Free Schools;

       
$ 876.6 million less for bilingual education.

The
President's reluctance to fulfill his programs has cost the 9th
Congressional District more than $54,000 in Title I funding alone.
	
	
	
	
	
	
	
	
 
	    	
		    
		    	Next Article

title: Congresswoman Barbara Lee and Congressional Pro-Choice Caucus Hold Press Conference for 31st Anniversary of Roe vs. Wade
text:
01.21.04
			 Congresswoman Barbara Lee and Congressional Pro-Choice Caucus Hold Press Conference for 31st Anniversary of Roe vs. Wade 
			Landmark
Case Decided January 22, 1973


Washington,
DC  Congresswoman Barbara Lee (D-CA) today joined colleagues from
the Congressional Pro-Choice Caucus to celebrate the landmark 1973 Roe
vs. Wade Supreme Court decision:  

We
commemorate the 31st anniversary of the Roe
vs. Wade decision tomorrow, a historic step in women's equal and
reproductive rights.  Even while this anniversary is an occasion for
celebration, it is also a date that reminds us that we have been kept from
realizing all that Roe promised. 
In short, the fundamental right of women to privacy and to control our own
bodies remains under fire.

As
the voice of the people, and especially as women, we must ensure that our
government doesn't undercut reproductive rights and sex education programs. 
We must ensure that woman around the world receive access to family planning and
vital HIV/AIDS prevention services they so desperately need  in short, we
must repeal the Bush Administration's Global Gag Rule.  We must ensure
that our government does not interfere with the FDA drug approval process.

And
we must fight to protect the judiciary from becoming more and more anti-choice.
 Freedom of choice has made a significant difference in the lives and
health of American women.  The most appropriate commemoration of the 31st
anniversary of the Roe v. Wade
anniversary involves a renewed commitment to preserving freedom of choice.

Pro-choice
Americans must stand up and make their voices heard if the right to choose is to
endure for future generations.  I pledge to uphold this Constitutional
right, and I challenge all lawmakers, the courts, and all Americans to do the
same.


                                                                       
###
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article

title: Congresswoman Barbara Lee Announces 9th Congressional District Appropriations Projects
text:
01.25.04
			 Congresswoman Barbara Lee Announces 9th Congressional District Appropriations Projects 
			Ed Roberts Campus to Receive $900,000    Oakland, CA  Following President Bush's signing of the FY 04 Omnibus Appropriations Bill over the weekend, Congresswoman Barbara Lee (D-CA) announced today that California's 9th Congressional District received more than $14.3 million in funding in the appropriations package.    Among the appropriations, Berkeley's Ed Roberts Campus will receive $900,000 toward the completion of what is to be the world's foremost center for disability right services, advocacy, education, training, and public policy.  I am very pleased that we could obtain this money for such a worthwhile cause as the Ed Roberts Campus, said Lee.  This money  and the campus  will be a tribute to Ed Roberts, who did so much to advance the ability of persons with disabilities to live independently. We are very pleased with Rep. Lee's continued support for the Ed Roberts Campus and the disability community, said Jan Garrett, ERC President and Executive Director of the Center for Independent Living. The Campus will be a national model for how non-profits working collaboratively can efficiently deliver services to people with disabilities in tough economic times. Other East Bay projects in the appropriations package include $5.65 million for a new Oakland Airport traffic control tower; $2.49 million for AC Transit to increase the number of its buses and enhance its CALWORKS program; $150,000 for a Unity Council parking facility in Fruitvale; and $100,000 for non-violence youth programs for the Martin Luther King, Jr. Freedom Center. ###    
	
	  

	
	
	
	
	
	
	

 
	    	  
		    
		    	Next Article
